mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over recent months, discount rates and funding stress ease quickly enough to lift equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of instances when FEDFUNDS has declined for 3 consecutive months, or rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A high policy-rate threshold indicates restrictive financial conditions that suppress valuation multiples and risk appetite, so SPX forward returns turn negative over the next 20 trading days once FEDFUNDS exceeds the stated level.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the average response is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and higher recession risk, which compresses equity expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months and the next 20 trading days of SPX returns are positive more often than negative, or if the signal's rolling 2-year hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth that slows while unemployment rises identifies a genuine labor-market deterioration; together they imply weaker consumption and profits, so SPX returns are negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of falling PAYEMS growth and rising UNRATE does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not below baseline in triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in retail sales indicates stronger final demand and revenue momentum, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if months with rising RSAFS do not lead to above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips negative in a 3-year rolling backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production expands while inflation is contained, firms gain real activity without immediate pricing pressure, improving margins and lifting SPX returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation is flat or falling over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if rising INDPRO during flat/falling CPIAUCSL fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not materially better than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts is an early cyclical warning that weakens construction, credit growth, and consumer confidence, so SPX returns turn negative over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drops more than 10% from its 3-month average and the next 5 trading days of SPX returns are not negative on average, or if the triggered hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP acceleration combined with stronger payroll growth identifies broad real-demand momentum that markets extrapolate into earnings strength, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous acceleration in GDPC1 and PAYEMS does not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal underperforms a simple market drift benchmark over any 4-year rolling window.
